---
title: "causal_analysis"
output: html_document
date: "2025-06-10"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

path = "C:/Users/USER/Documents/empirical_evidence_corren"

# Set the working directory for the whole document
# knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = path)
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = here::here(path))
```

```{r include=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(broom)
library(ggpubr)
library(tidyr)
library(gtable)
library(grid) # for unit.pmax()
library(gridExtra)
library(lubridate)
library(stargazer)
library(vtable)
library(stringr)
library(lmtest)
library(sandwich)
library(purrr)
library("readxl")
library(here)
library(haven)
library(scales)
library(fixest)
library(fst)
```


```{r include=FALSE}
#load(file=paste(path,"data", "df_withempirical.RData",sep = '/'))


out <- read_fst(paste(here(),"data","out_hearings_enriched.fst",sep = '/'))

df <- out

unique_years <- sort(unique(na.omit(df$year)), decreasing = FALSE)
unique_congress <- sort(unique(na.omit(df$congress)), decreasing = FALSE)

custom_ticks <- unique_years[seq(1, length(unique_years), by = 2)]  # Select every 2nd year
custom_ticks <- seq(1996, 2016, by = 2)


# Define Congress sessions and their corresponding years
congress_mapping <- data.frame(
  congress = unique_congress,
  start_year = seq(1997, by = 2, length.out = 10),
  end_year = seq(1998, by = 2, length.out = 10)
)

# Generate custom ticks for the x-axis
custom_labels <- paste0(custom_ticks, " (", df$congress[match(custom_ticks, df$year)],"th)")







```



```{r include=FALSE}

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Relative-yea variables (unchanged)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
df <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(year)) %>%                     # safety
  mutate(
    rel_year_2007 = year - 2007,
    rel_year_2011 = year - 2011
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Event-study regressions, restricted to ±4 years
#    (use the `subset =` argument of feols)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
event_party_2007 <- feols(
  mean_score ~ i(rel_year_2007, dem, ref = -1) |
    thomas_name + year,
  data   = df,
  subset = ~ abs(rel_year_2007) <= 3          # ← note the tilde
)

event_party_2011 <- feols(
  mean_score ~ i(rel_year_2011, dem, ref = -1) |
    thomas_name + year,
  data   = df,
  subset = ~ abs(rel_year_2011) <= 3
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Plot helper: convert relative breaks to calendar-year labels
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
make_year_axis <- function(rel_breaks, flip_year) {
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks  = rel_breaks,
    labels  = rel_breaks + flip_year,         # convert to calendar
    name    = "Calendar year"
  )
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Plot: 2007 Democratic flip
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2007 flip plot
# Extract coefficients as a data frame
coef_df_2007 <- broom::tidy(event_party_2007, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  filter(grepl("^rel_year_2007", term))

# Extract relative year + party info
coef_df_2007 <- coef_df_2007 %>%
  mutate(
    rel_year = as.numeric(sub("rel_year_2007::(-?\\d+):.*", "\\1", term)),
    party = sub(".*:(0|1)$", "\\1", term),
    party = ifelse(party == "1", "Democrat", "Republican")
  )

# Now ggplot
p2007 <- ggplot(coef_df_2007, aes(x = rel_year + 2007, y = estimate)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high), width = 0.2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 2004:2010) +
  labs(
    x = "Calendar year",
    y = "Effect on Empirical Discourse"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()



coef_df_2011 <- broom::tidy(event_party_2011, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  filter(grepl("^rel_year_2011", term)) %>%
  mutate(
    rel_year = as.numeric(sub("rel_year_2011::(-?\\d+):.*", "\\1", term)),
    party = sub(".*:(0|1)$", "\\1", term),
    party = ifelse(party == "1", "Democrat", "Republican")
  )

p2011 <- ggplot(coef_df_2011, aes(x = rel_year + 2011, y = estimate)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high), width = 0.2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 2008:2014) +
  labs(
    x = "Calendar year",
    y = "Effect on Empirical Discourse")+
  theme_minimal()



# Save the plot
ggsave(file.path(here(), "plots", "event_study_plot_2007.png"),
       plot = p2007, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 150)

ggsave(file.path(here(), "plots", "event_study_plot_2011.png"),
       plot = p2011, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 150)


# identify surviors


count_survivors <- function(df, flip_year, rel_var) {

  df %>%
    mutate(rel_year = .data[[rel_var]]) %>%
    filter(abs(rel_year) <= 3) %>%              # match estimation window
    group_by(thomas_name) %>%
    summarise(
      party = first(dem),                       # party is time-invariant here
      pre  = any(rel_year < 0),
      post = any(rel_year >= 0),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    filter(pre & post) %>%                      # survivors
    count(party) %>%
    mutate(
      party = ifelse(party == 1, "Democrat", "Republican"),
      flip_year = flip_year
    )
}

survivors_2007 <- count_survivors(df, 2007, "rel_year_2007")
survivors_2011 <- count_survivors(df, 2011, "rel_year_2011")

survivors <- bind_rows(survivors_2007, survivors_2011)



count_exposed <- function(df, flip_year, rel_var) {

  df %>%
    mutate(rel_year = .data[[rel_var]]) %>%
    filter(abs(rel_year) <= 3) %>%
    group_by(thomas_name) %>%
    summarise(
      party = first(dem),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    count(party) %>%
    mutate(
      party = ifelse(party == 1, "Democrat", "Republican"),
      flip_year = flip_year
    )
}

exposed <- bind_rows(
  count_exposed(df, 2007, "rel_year_2007"),
  count_exposed(df, 2011, "rel_year_2011")
)

survivors

exposed


```

```{r include=FALSE}

# Estimate model
event_party <- feols(
  mean_score ~ i(year, dem, ref = 2006) | thomas_name + year,
  data = df
)

# Extract tidy results
tidy_event <- broom::tidy(event_party, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  filter(grepl("year::", term)) %>%
  mutate(
    year = as.numeric(sub("year::(\\d{4}).*", "\\1", term)),
    party = "Democrat"
  )


g_event_study <- ggplot(tidy_event, aes(x = year, y = estimate)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_point(aes(x = 2006, y = 0), shape = 21, size = 1.5, fill = "gray", stroke = 0.5)+ # reference year

  # geom_line() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high), width = 0.2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(2007, 2011), linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  annotate("text", x = 2007, y = max(tidy_event$estimate, na.rm = TRUE) + 0.01,
           label = "Dem House Majority", angle = 90, vjust = -0.4, size = 3.5) +
  annotate("text", x = 2011, y = max(tidy_event$estimate, na.rm = TRUE) + 0.01,
           label = "GOP House Majority", angle = 90, vjust = -0.4, size = 3.5) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = sort(unique(c(seq(min(tidy_event$year), max(tidy_event$year), by = 1), 2007, 2011)))
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Event Study: Minority Status Effect by Party",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Effect on Empirical Score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()



ggsave(
    filename = paste0(here(), "/plots/event_study_plot.png"),
    plot = g_event_study,
    width = 8,
    height = 6,
    dpi = 300
  )



```



```{r include=FALSE}


# Identify members who served all years 2003–2011
eligible_members <- df %>%
  filter(year %in% 2005:2009) %>%
  group_by(thomas_name) %>%
  summarise(years_active = n_distinct(year)) %>%
  filter(years_active == 4)  # 2003–2011 inclusive



# Subset full data
df_subset <- df %>%
  filter(thomas_name %in% eligible_members$thomas_name) %>%
  mutate(post_2007 = ifelse(year >= 2007, 1, 0))


model <- feols(
  mean_score ~ post_2007 * dem | thomas_name,
  data = df_subset
)


tm <- tidy(model, conf.int = TRUE)

# Extract relevant coefficients
rep_est      <- tm$estimate[tm$term == "post_2007"]
rep_low      <- tm$conf.low[tm$term == "post_2007"]
rep_high     <- tm$conf.high[tm$term == "post_2007"]

dem_diff     <- tm$estimate[tm$term == "post_2007:dem"]
dem_low_diff <- tm$conf.low[tm$term == "post_2007:dem"]
dem_high_diff<- tm$conf.high[tm$term == "post_2007:dem"]

# Compute Democrat total effect
dem_est  <- rep_est + dem_diff
dem_low  <- rep_low + dem_low_diff
dem_high <- rep_high + dem_high_diff

# Assemble data frame for plotting
tidy_model <- tibble(
  party = c("Republican", "Democrat"),
  estimate = c(rep_est, dem_est),
  conf.low = c(rep_low, dem_low),
  conf.high = c(rep_high, dem_high)
)

g_2007 <- ggplot(tidy_model, aes(x = party, y = estimate, fill = party)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.6, color = "black") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high), width = 0.2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    title = "Effect of 2007 House Flip on Empirical Discourse by Party",
    y = "Estimated Change in Empirical Score",
    x = NULL
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Democrat" = "blue", "Republican" = "red")) +
  theme_minimal()



ggsave(
    filename = paste0(here(), "/plots/flip_effects_2007.png"),
    plot = g_2007,
    width = 8,
    height = 6,
    dpi = 300
  )





# Identify members who served all years
eligible_members <- df %>%
  filter(year %in% 2009:2013) %>%
  group_by(thomas_name) %>%
  summarise(years_active = n_distinct(year)) %>%
  filter(years_active == 4)  # 2003–2011 inclusive

# Subset full data
df_subset <- df %>%
  filter(thomas_name %in% eligible_members$thomas_name) %>%
  mutate(post_2011 = ifelse(year >= 2011, 1, 0))



model <- feols(
  mean_score ~ post_2011 * dem | thomas_name,
  data = df_subset
)


tm <- tidy(model, conf.int = TRUE)

# Extract relevant coefficients
rep_est      <- tm$estimate[tm$term == "post_2011"]
rep_low      <- tm$conf.low[tm$term == "post_2011"]
rep_high     <- tm$conf.high[tm$term == "post_2011"]

dem_diff     <- tm$estimate[tm$term == "post_2011:dem"]
dem_low_diff <- tm$conf.low[tm$term == "post_2011:dem"]
dem_high_diff<- tm$conf.high[tm$term == "post_2011:dem"]

# Compute Democrat total effect
dem_est  <- rep_est + dem_diff
dem_low  <- rep_low + dem_low_diff
dem_high <- rep_high + dem_high_diff

# Assemble data frame for plotting
tidy_model <- tibble(
  party = c("Republican", "Democrat"),
  estimate = c(rep_est, dem_est),
  conf.low = c(rep_low, dem_low),
  conf.high = c(rep_high, dem_high)
)

g_2011 <- ggplot(tidy_model, aes(x = party, y = estimate, fill = party)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.6, color = "black") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high), width = 0.2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    title = "Effect of 2011 House Flip on Empirical Discourse by Party",
    y = "Estimated Change in Empirical Score",
    x = NULL
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Democrat" = "blue", "Republican" = "red")) +
  theme_minimal()



ggsave(
    filename = paste0(here(), "/plots/flip_effects_2011.png"),
    plot = g_2011,
    width = 8,
    height = 6,
    dpi = 300
  )


```
